name: Herb Pizza Dough
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Garlic olive oil (more as needed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir together the yeast, sugar and warm water. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a work bowl fitted with the steel blade of a food processor, chop the herbs. Turn off the machine.
3. Add flour and salt to the chopped herbs. Turn the machine on and run for a few seconds.
4. While the machine is running, gradually add the yeast mixture. Process until the dough forms a ball at the side of the bowl.
5. Add garlic oil and process for 30-40 seconds more.
6. Transfer the dough to an oiled bowl. Turn the dough over so the entire surface is coated with oil. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free place for 1 hour or until doubled.
7. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough. If it's too elastic, toss it between your hands to flatten it slightly.
8. Lightly grease the pizza pan with oil and sprinkle with cornmeal. Place the dough on the pan and trim the edges.
9. Bake at 425°F for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and lightly brush the crust with more oil before proceeding with the recipe.
Makes enough dough for one 12-inch crust.
Try basil, thyme, Italian flat leaf parsley, oregano, rosemary, cilantro.
